title: Echidnopsis framesii

Traditionally, Echidnopsis has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Echidnopsis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Echidnopsis is often used in herbal preparations such as capsules, extracts, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support mental health and well-being.

The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, often referred to as dogbane or oleander family.
